annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Niri-style scrollable-tiling control. The KWin effect reports window lifecycle for screens in scroll mode here; the daemon forwards each event to the ScrollEngine. Window geometry flows back to the effect over org.plasmazones.WindowTracking (applyGeometriesBatch), shared with the other placement modes.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Whether scroll mode is active on any screen (read-only, derived from the engine's any-screen-active state).

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: List of screen IDs currently using scroll mode. The effect subscribes to scrollScreensChanged so it knows whose windows to report on this interface.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Emitted when the master scroll-mode gate flips (any-screen-active → none, or vice versa).

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: New enabled state.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Emitted when the set of screens using scroll mode changes.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: List of screen IDs currently using scroll mode.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Notify the daemon that a window appeared on a scroll-mode screen.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Window ID from KWin.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Screen where the window appeared.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Batch form of windowOpened used on daemon startup / scroll-mode toggle-on to avoid per-window D-Bus round-trips. The (ssii) entry struct is shared with org.plasmazones.Autotile; scroll's strip model is size-agnostic, so the trailing minWidth/minHeight ints are ignored (non-resizable windows are fitted to the tile slot effect-side). SECONDARY ROLE — restore reconcile signal: the FIRST batch the effect sends after a (re)connect doubles as the live-window reconcile for any strip restored from scroll-session.json. The daemon treats it as the complete live scroll-window set; restored windows that the batch does not confirm are pruned (their column dropped when it empties), so the empty batch case is meaningful and MUST be sent. Subsequent batches are routine and the reconcile path no-ops.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Array of (windowId, screenId, minWidth, minHeight) entries; minWidth/minHeight are ignored by scroll.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Notify the daemon that a scroll-mode window was closed.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Window ID from KWin.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Notify the daemon that a scroll-mode window gained focus. Updates the engine's active column / tile and triggers a viewport scroll if the new focus falls outside the visible viewport.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Window ID that gained focus.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Screen where the focused window is located.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Notify the daemon that a scroll-mode window was minimized (true) or restored (false). A minimized window keeps its slot in the strip but drops out of the visible layout.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Window ID whose minimized state changed.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: True if the window was minimized, false if restored.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Notify the daemon that a scroll-mode window was drag-released (drag-to-reorder). Its column is moved next to the anchor window's column — before it, or after when placeAfter is true. The effect picks the anchor as the on-strip window nearest the drop point.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Window ID being dragged.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: Window ID of the on-strip anchor nearest the drop point.

annotation.name: org.gtk.GDBus.DocString
annotation.value: True to place the dragged column after the anchor's column; false to place it before.
